metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state via MongoDB (metadata) and Redis (caching/rate-limiting). It exposes full CRUD operations on S3 objects and buckets (PUT, GET, DELETE, POST). User-specific data is managed through access keys and accounts. Classified as stateful-crud.

findings.title: No Network Security Configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation definitions exist in this repository. Health checks are IP-allowlisted but no infrastructure-level network isolation is defined.
findings.gap: No network security infrastructure defined. Cannot verify that the application runs in private subnets with proper segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets, least-privilege security groups, and network segmentation in IaC. Consider VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists in this repository. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize manifests found.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. Infrastructure changes are managed outside this codebase with no visibility or version control alongside the application.
findings.recommendation: Adopt CDK (TypeScript) or Terraform to define EKS cluster, DocumentDB/ElastiCache, VPC/networking, ALB, auto-scaling, and backup plans. Co-locate IaC with application code.

findings.title: No API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or load balancer configuration exists. The S3 server listens directly on port 8000.
findings.gap: No managed entry point with throttling, authentication offload, or request validation.
findings.recommendation: Deploy behind an ALB or API Gateway. Configure throttling, health-check-based routing, and TLS termination.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration found. The application supports cluster mode for multi-process execution but no HPA or ASG definitions exist.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling mechanisms. Static capacity provisioning cannot respond to traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Define Kubernetes HPA on EKS based on request rate and latency metrics. Configure auto-scaling for managed databases.

findings.title: No Backup and Recovery Configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration found. MongoDB and Redis have no automated backup plans, retention policies, or PITR configurations defined.
findings.gap: No backup or recovery configuration. Data loss protection depends entirely on external infrastructure management.
findings.recommendation: Enable automated backups on DocumentDB with PITR. Configure ElastiCache automatic backups. Define an AWS Backup plan.

findings.title: No Multi-AZ Configuration
findings.description: No multi-AZ deployment configuration exists in this repository. MongoDB replica set and Redis Sentinel support suggest HA intent but actual AZ distribution is not configured here.
findings.gap: No multi-AZ deployment verified. HA depends on external infrastructure configuration.
findings.recommendation: Configure EKS node groups across 3 AZs. DocumentDB supports multi-AZ automatically. Configure ElastiCache multi-AZ failover.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No distributed tracing instrumented. No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or Jaeger. Request correlation uses werelogs request IDs but does not propagate across service boundaries.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. Debugging request flows across CloudServer, MongoDB, Redis, Vault, and Backbeat requires manual log correlation.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry SDK for Node.js. Export traces to AWS X-Ray. Propagate W3C traceparent headers.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident response automation, or self-healing patterns found. Alerting fires notifications but no automated response exists.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No structured runbooks for common incidents.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for each alert condition. Start with Markdown runbooks, then automate with Step Functions or SSM Automation.

findings.title: Self-Managed Databases
findings.description: MongoDB replica set and Redis are self-managed. No managed database services (DocumentDB, ElastiCache) referenced.
findings.gap: All databases self-managed. Operational burden includes manual patching, backup, and scaling.
findings.recommendation: Migrate MongoDB to DocumentDB or DynamoDB (preferred). Migrate Redis to ElastiCache or MemoryDB.

findings.title: Hardcoded Workflow Orchestration
findings.description: Multi-step operations (multipart uploads, lifecycle) are implemented as in-code state machines without dedicated orchestration.
findings.gap: Workflow logic embedded in application code without dedicated orchestration for error handling and retries.
findings.recommendation: Consider AWS Step Functions for multi-step operations. Extend the existing Backbeat delegation pattern.

findings.title: Monolithic Application
findings.description: Single deployable unit with identifiable modules (api, data, metadata, kms, auth) sharing a single process, config, and direct in-process function imports.
findings.gap: Modules cannot be independently scaled, deployed, or replaced. Cross-module coupling through shared config and direct imports.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig decomposition. Extract metadata service first (already has wrapper interface).

findings.title: Limited API Versioning
findings.description: The S3 API does not use URL-path versioning (follows S3 protocol convention). No versioning strategy for extensions or internal APIs.
findings.gap: No explicit versioning for custom extensions or management APIs.
findings.recommendation: Document S3 API version compatibility. Implement version headers for custom extensions.

findings.title: Static Service Endpoints
findings.description: Service endpoints configured via environment variables without dynamic service discovery. Changes require container restart.
findings.gap: No dynamic service discovery. All endpoints statically configured.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Kubernetes service DNS. Consider AWS Cloud Map for cross-cluster discovery during microservice decomposition.

findings.title: Database Versions Not Pinned in IaC
findings.description: MongoDB driver version pinned in package.json but server engine version not pinned in infrastructure configuration. No documented version-update procedure.
findings.gap: Database server engine versions not explicitly pinned. No version-update procedure documented.
findings.recommendation: Pin database engine versions in IaC. Document upgrade procedure. Managed services handle this automatically.

findings.title: Proprietary Identity Provider
findings.description: Uses Scality Vault as IdP rather than a standard centralized identity provider. No OIDC/SAML federation or SSO configured.
findings.gap: Proprietary IdP without standard federation capabilities.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito or configure OIDC federation between Scality Vault and AWS IAM.

findings.title: No Dedicated Secrets Management
findings.description: Secrets managed via Docker secrets, GitHub Secrets, and environment variables. No AWS Secrets Manager or rotation configured. Config.json contains placeholder credentials.
findings.gap: No dedicated secrets management service with rotation. Production credentials likely in environment variables without encryption.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Secrets Manager for all production credentials. Configure automatic rotation. Use IAM roles where possible.

findings.title: Limited Compute Hardening
findings.description: Slim base image used (node:22-bookworm-slim) with multi-stage build. No container image vulnerability scanning in CI. Legacy DockerfileMem uses EOL Node.js 6.
findings.gap: No container vulnerability scanning. No hardened base image. Legacy Dockerfile uses EOL runtime.
findings.recommendation: Add Trivy container scanning to CI. Remove DockerfileMem (node:6 EOL). Consider Bottlerocket for production.

findings.title: No Formal SLO Definitions
findings.description: Prometheus alert thresholds function as informal SLO-adjacent signals but are not formalized as SLO/SLI definitions with error budgets.
findings.gap: Operational thresholds exist as alert rules but not formalized as SLOs with error budgets.
findings.recommendation: Formalize alert thresholds as SLOs: availability (99.9%), latency (p99 < 500ms), error rate (< 3% 5xx). Implement error budget tracking.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: Grafana dashboard and alerting rules exist but no CODEOWNERS, named alarm owners, team attribution, or documented escalation paths.
findings.gap: Observability assets lack ownership attribution.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS for monitoring/. Add owner labels to alerts. Document escalation paths.

findings.title: Limited Resource Tagging
findings.description: Docker image labels exist (git.repository, git.commit-sha) but no AWS resource tagging strategy. No cost allocation, environment, or ownership tags.
findings.gap: No AWS resource tagging strategy. Docker labels exist but no infrastructure resource tags.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter). Apply required tags in IaC. Configure AWS Config enforcement.

findings.title: Container Orchestration Managed Externally
findings.description: Application is containerized and deployed to Kubernetes but K8s manifests and EKS configuration are managed externally.
findings.gap: Cannot confirm whether EKS (managed) or self-managed Kubernetes is used. Deployment config not co-located.
findings.recommendation: Adopt EKS (preferred). Co-locate Helm charts with application code for deployment transparency.

findings.title: CI Automated but No Deployment Pipeline
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides comprehensive CI (lint, test, build, security scanning). No deployment automation — deployment to Kubernetes handled externally.
findings.gap: CI/CD covers build and test but not deployment. No automated deployment pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Extend CI/CD with ArgoCD for GitOps deployment on EKS. Add deployment verification.

findings.title: S3-Compatible Storage Without Parsing Pipeline
findings.description: The application IS an S3-compatible object storage server. Data stored in S3-compatible format but no content parsing pipeline exists.
findings.gap: No automated parsing or content extraction for stored objects.
findings.recommendation: Content parsing should be a downstream consumer concern. Integrate Textract or S3 Object Lambda if content analysis is needed.

findings.title: Well-Structured Data Access with Minor Gaps
findings.description: Wrapper pattern provides unified data access (metadata/wrapper.js, data/wrapper.js, kms/wrapper.js). Minor inconsistency: Redis accessed directly for rate limiting.
findings.gap: Minor: Some Redis access bypasses centralized abstractions.
findings.recommendation: Consider a unified cache abstraction wrapper for Redis access consistency.

findings.title: No Stored Procedures — Minor Migration Concerns
findings.description: No stored procedures, triggers, or proprietary SQL. All business logic in application layer. MongoDB-specific query patterns may need adjustment for DocumentDB/DynamoDB.
findings.gap: Minor: Some MongoDB-specific patterns may need adjustment during migration.
findings.recommendation: Verify MongoDB query pattern compatibility with DocumentDB. For DynamoDB, redesign data access patterns.

findings.title: Multiple KMS Backends Without Rotation Policy
findings.description: Comprehensive SSE with 5 KMS backends (mem, file, Scality, KMIP, AWS KMS). Default encryption per account supported. No evidence of key rotation policy.
findings.gap: KMS config has placeholder credentials. No documented key rotation policy.
findings.recommendation: Use AWS KMS with CMK and automatic rotation. Remove placeholder credentials. Use IAM roles for KMS access.

findings.title: S3 SigV4 Auth Without OAuth2/JWT
findings.description: AWS Signature V4 authentication on all S3 API requests via Vault integration. Appropriate for S3 protocol but internal/management APIs may lack equivalent auth rigor.
findings.gap: Auth is S3-specific (SigV4). Internal/management APIs may not have equivalent protection.
findings.recommendation: For non-S3 internal APIs, add OAuth2/JWT. Ensure Vault integration is used in production (not in-memory auth).

findings.title: SAST and Dependency Scanning Without Container Scanning
findings.description: CodeQL (SAST) for JS/Python/Ruby and dependency-review-action for vulnerability scanning are in CI. No container scanning or blocking security gate.
findings.gap: No container image scanning. No explicit blocking gate on critical findings.
findings.recommendation: Add container scanning (Trivy). Configure CodeQL to block on critical findings. Consider DAST.

findings.title: Business Metrics Present but Incomplete
findings.description: Business-relevant metrics published (buckets_count, objects_count, ingested_bytes, quota metrics). UTAPI provides per-account usage. Lacks business outcome correlation.
findings.gap: Business metrics exist for storage operations but lack correlation to business outcomes.
findings.recommendation: Extend metrics to include per-customer request rates, storage growth trends, SLA compliance tracking.

findings.title: Static Threshold Alerting Only
findings.description: Multi-level static threshold alerting (warning + critical) covers endpoint availability, error rates, and latency. No anomaly detection configured.
findings.gap: All alerting uses static thresholds. No anomaly detection for gradual degradation or novel failures.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch anomaly detection for key metrics. Consider Prometheus-based z-score alerting.

findings.title: Extensive Functional Tests Without Contract Testing
findings.description: 224 functional test files covering AWS SDK, multi-backend, MongoDB, KMIP, Python boto, Java, Ruby. Run in CI against real services in Docker Compose.
findings.gap: No contract tests for external dependencies (Vault, Backbeat, bucketd).
findings.recommendation: Add contract tests (Pact) for Vault, Backbeat, and bucketd interfaces. Critical during decomposition.

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, subnet, or security group definitions found in this repo
top_gaps.impact: Cannot verify network isolation; services may be exposed without proper segmentation

top_gaps.title: IaC Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No Infrastructure as Code found in this repository
top_gaps.impact: Infrastructure changes are manual, non-reproducible, and error-prone

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing instrumented (no OpenTelemetry, X-Ray)
top_gaps.impact: Debugging failures across service boundaries is guesswork

top_gaps.title: Incident Response
top_gaps.gap: No runbooks or automated incident response found
top_gaps.impact: Incident response is entirely ad hoc with no structured playbooks

top_gaps.title: API Entry Point
top_gaps.gap: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront entry point defined
top_gaps.impact: Services lack throttling, auth offload, and centralized traffic management

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3). GitHub Actions workflows automate build, test, lint, and Docker image publishing.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, monitors workflow runs, and manages release processes via the GitHub Actions API.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose workflow dispatch endpoints and build status APIs for agent consumption.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (README.md, DESIGN.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, TESTING.md, Healthchecks.md, docs/ directory).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes documentation and answers developer questions about S3 API implementation, deployment, and configuration.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index Markdown and RST documentation files into a vector store.

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Comprehensive Prometheus metrics and alerting rules exist (OPS-Q3=3, OPS-Q4=3).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that queries Prometheus metrics, correlates alert firings, and suggests root causes.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Add distributed tracing for full cross-service correlation. Currently limited to metrics-only.

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.description: Incrementally extract services while keeping the monolith running.

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.description: Selectively extract high-value modules based on business priority.

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.description: Recommended against. The monolith is functional with clear module boundaries.

decomposition_strategy.first_extraction: Metadata service (lib/metadata/) — well-defined wrapper interface, MongoDB backend, minimal dependencies.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (monolith with identifiable modules but coupling)

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared process and cross-module coupling

immediate_actions: Identify service boundaries from existing module structure

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=3; container definitions already present (8 Dockerfiles, docker-compose). Application is already containerized.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1=3 meets threshold; application already containerized and deployed to Kubernetes

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Application is already containerized (multi-stage Dockerfile, deployed to Kubernetes). Container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (no stored procedures); no commercial DB engines detected (MongoDB is open source).

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4=3 meets threshold; no proprietary SQL

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. MongoDB is open source. DATA-Q4=3 meets threshold.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=2 (self-managed MongoDB/Redis), DATA-Q3=2 (no version pinning in IaC)

triggering_questions.note: Database engine versions not pinned in IaC

immediate_actions: Assess MongoDB query pattern compatibility with DocumentDB

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=4 (sync is correct design for stateful-crud archetype); no data processing workloads detected in repository.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q4=4; sync communication is architecturally correct for this service

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads detected. INF-Q4=4 (appropriate sync design). Contextual guard: no streaming/ETL/analytics artifacts found.

triggering_questions.note: No Infrastructure as Code in repository

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context ('Scality open-source S3-compatible object-storage server') does not contain AI-related signal terms.
